mission.title: argo: Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph)
mission.description: Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph). This issue affects 132+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo feature symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app=argo
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors related to: Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph)

steps.title: Check current Service configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo resources:
```bash
kubectl get service -A
kubectl describe service <name> -n argo
```
When defining Application please allow a new field that is very similar to SyncWaves but allows for more fine-grained control of dependencies, thus allowing more parallelism.  For any Application, I

steps.title: Apply the fix for Please allow for defining dependency DAG (Directed Acyclic
steps.description:
PoC for #3517 - up for discussion. Things to note:

* Currently, manual syncing is also blocked when there are unmet dependencies
* Not a single test yet

Credit where credit is due: Dependency resolution was taken from http://dnaeon.github.io/dependency-graph-resolution-algorithm-in-go/

Enables
```yaml
syncWaves:
    clusterBootstrap:
      namespaces:
        platformTopology: 0
        playgroundTopology: 0
      priorityClasses: 0
      storageClasses: 0
      sealedSecretsController: 10
      sealedSecrets: 20
      spotTerminationHandler: 10
    istio:
      controlPlane: 1000
      kiali: 1010
    infra:
      jaeger: 2000
      prometheus:
        operator: 2000
        alertRules: 2010
        grafanaDashboard: 2010
        thanos: 2010
      certManager: 2000
      argoCd: 2100
    operators:
      spark: 3000
      zookeeper: 3000
    knative:
      operat
```

steps.title: Confirm Please allow for defining dependency DAG is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo -n argo --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The root cause is: PoC for #3517 - up for discussion. Things to note:

* Currently, manual syncing is also blocked when there are unmet dependencies
* Not a single test yet

Credit where credit is due: Dependency resolution was taken from http://dnaeon.github.io/dependency-graph-resolution-algorithm-in-go/

Enables to specify dependencies between `Applications`:

```
dependsOn:
  - applicationName: otherApp
  -.
codeSnippets:
syncWaves:
    clusterBootstrap:
      namespaces:
        platformTopology: 0
        playgroundTopology: 0
      priorityClasses: 0
      storageClasses: 0
      sealedSecretsController: 10
      sealedSecrets: 20
      spotTerminationHandler: 10
    istio:
      controlPlane: 1000
      kiali: 1010
    infra:
      jaeger: 2000
      prometheus:
        operator: 2000
        alertRules: 2010
        grafanaDashboard: 2010
        thanos: 2010
      certManager: 2000
      argoCd: 2100
    operators:
      spark: 3000
      zookeeper: 3000
    knative:
      operator: 3000
      serving: 4000
      eventing: 4000
      source:
        config: 3000
        sqs: 4000
      kafka:
        zookeeper: 4000
    search:
      core:
        zookeeper: 5000

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo installed or the issue environment reproducible.
